## Our Prices
SizePrice (BDT)Availability
250g৳320In stock
500g৳600In stock
1kg৳1,100In stock
*Prices valid as of 15 May 2026. Subject to seasonal change.* The 1kg jar offers the best per-gram value at ৳1.10/g, compared to ৳1.28/g for the 250g jar. If your family uses honey regularly, the larger size saves money over time.

## Price Drivers Authentic Sundarbans Kholisha honey costs more than generic supermarket honey for real, structural reasons — not branding.
Cost FactorWhy It Matters
Forest access permitsCollectors (Mouwalis) must purchase seasonal permits from the Bangladesh Forest Department before entering the Sundarbans. These are limited and add a direct cost before any honey is collected.
Dangerous collection workMouwalis travel deep into the forest by boat and collect from wild hives in an active tiger and crocodile habitat. That occupational risk is reflected in fair collector earnings.
Short harvest windowKholisha flowers bloom for only 6–8 weeks a year, between February and April. The entire year's supply comes from this one window — there is no way to produce more if demand increases.
Low, unpredictable yieldWild collection depends entirely on what nature provides each season. Collectors cannot increase output by adding managed hives or supplemental feeding.
Transport from a remote locationHoney travels by boat from deep forest collection points to depots in Mongla or Khulna, then overland to Narayanganj. Each step carries a real logistics cost.
Raw handlingWe source honey that has not been heated or pasteurised. Keeping it raw requires more careful storage and faster stock turnover — both cost more than heat-treating the product to artificially extend shelf life.

## Market Rate Range Honey prices in Bangladesh vary widely. Here is an honest picture of what different product types cost, based on general market observation as of 2025–2026.
Product TypeApprox. Market Price (BDT/kg)What You're Actually Getting
Generic commercial honey (retail shops)300 – 600Farmed colonies, usually heat-treated, mixed floral sources, adulteration common
"Sundarbans honey" with no sourcing detail600 – 900Label claim only; origin and quality cannot be verified
Authentic Kholisha honey (mid-supply chain)900 – 1,400Some traceability; generally reliable quality
Hasanah Mart Kholisha honey1,100 (per kg jar)Traceable sourcing, raw and unheated, single floral source
Our 1kg price of ৳1,100 sits at the accessible end of the authentic Kholisha honey range. We work to keep our prices fair without compromising on sourcing quality. **One honest warning for your family's protection:** if another seller is offering "authentic Sundarbans Kholisha honey" for under ৳700/kg, the numbers simply do not add up. Real collection costs cannot support that price. When a deal looks too good, the honey is usually not what it claims to be.

## Value Justification

We understand ৳320 for a 250g jar feels like more than you'd pay for honey at a local shop. Here is what that price gets your family — plainly stated.

**What you get with Hasanah Mart's Kholisha honey:**

- **One clear floral source.** Your honey comes from Kholisha bloom specifically, not a blend of whatever was cheapest to collect that season.
- **Raw and unheated.** Naturally occurring enzymes and pollen are intact. Most commercial honey is pasteurised, which destroys these.
- **No added sugars or fillers.** Cheap honey is frequently cut with sugar syrup, rice syrup, or glucose solution. What we sell is just honey.
- **A traceable origin.** In a market where adulteration is widespread, knowing your honey came from a real, documented source in the Sundarbans has genuine value.

**What it is not:**

Honey is food, not medicine. We will not make health claims we cannot back up. What we can say honestly is that your family is getting a clean, natural, minimally processed food product from one of Bangladesh's most protected ecosystems — and that is worth a fair price.

</section>

## Cost vs Alternatives
ProductApprox. Price (BDT/kg)Key Difference
Refined white sugar120 – 140Pure sucrose only; no enzymes, pollen, or natural compounds
Commercial farmed honey (local brands)400 – 600Managed colonies, multi-floral, usually heat-treated, adulteration risk
Imported Manuka or Sidr honey2,000 – 5,000+Foreign origin, internationally certified, high import premium
Hasanah Mart Kholisha honey1,100 (1kg jar)Wild-harvested, raw, single-source, locally produced, no import cost
Imported premium honeys like New Zealand Manuka or Yemeni Sidr carry internationally recognised certifications and command very high prices. Authentic Kholisha honey offers comparable qualities — wild-harvested, raw, single-origin — grown right here in Bangladesh, without the import markup. At Hasanah Mart, we source carefully so your family gets honest value: a real product, at a fair price, delivered to your door.
